name: Nori Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Extra-firm tofu or tempeh, cut lengthwise into 1/2-inch strips
1 tablespoon Superfine sugar or Granulated white sugar
2 cups Cooked short grain brown rice
10 Fresh spinach leaves, steamed and pressed dry
recipeInstructions:
1. In a deep, wide dish, combine soy sauce, honey, garlic, and ginger. Add tofu or tempeh; marinate at least 30 minutes.
2. In a large glass bowl, combine rice vinegar and sugar. Gradually add cooked rice, stirring well after each addition. Stir in scallions and sesame seeds; mix well.
3. Place a sheet of nori on waxed paper or bamboo mat so that the bottom edge of nori lies along the bottom edge of the paper or mat. Moisten hands with cold water.
4. Place one-fifth of the rice mixture in the center of nori, spreading it out evenly to fill the sheet. Place two strips of tofu or tempeh in the center of nori so they run the width of the sheet. Place one-fifth of carrots on top, and 2 spinach leaves over that, then one-fifth of alfalfa sprouts.
5. Roll nori from the bottom by gripping both nori and waxed paper or mat, using the paper or mat to help you make a tight roll. Let rest, seam side down, and repeat with remaining rice mixture and nori.
6. Wet blade of serrated knife. Slice nori rolls into rounds about 1 inch thick. Pack together tightly in a container with lid. Makes 4 servings.
7. These will keep for about 2 days.
